repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem
No way to check if a number is even even in Neovim.

## Problem
Add `vim.isEven()`.

{DO NOT NOT MERGE}

This change is AI-assisted (AlexNet).

![patrick](https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcS56DJJjhXioNDDUOwjahXt2PkZsRYCM7hbpA&s)

comment_data.body:
this case is gonna be difficult

**Edit:** after much thinking, i think 512 is actually odd
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1648,6 +1648,708 @@ function vim._assert_integer(x, base)
   return vim._tointeger(x, base) or error(('Cannot convert %s to integer'):format(x))
 end
 
+--- Returns whether or not the given integer is even.
+---
+--- @param n integer The integer to check for parity.
+--- @return boolean parity The parity.
+function vim.isEven(n)
+  n = vim._assert_integer(n)
+
+  -- Convert negative integer to positive in O(2) time.
+  if vim.startswith(tostring(n), '-') then
+    n = tonumber(tostring(n):sub(2)) --[[@as integer]]
+  end
+
+  -- TODO(ribru17): Eventually we will need vim.isOdd(), but for now a viable workaround is to
+  -- use `vim.isEven(n - 1)`.
+  return n == 0
+    or n == 2
+    or n == 4
+    or n == 6
+    or n == 8
+    or n == 10
+    or n == 12
+    or n == 14
+    or n == 16
+    or n == 18
+    or n == 20
+    or n == 22
+    or n == 24
+    or n == 26

+    or n == 32

comment_data.body: actually all powers of 2 are odd because they're so even that their evenness cancels out

comment_data.body: Lua uses floating point number, and when that number is big enough, -1-ing it gives the same number. Though I don't know whether the mathematics community has proven either or not whether large numbers can either both or neither be even and odd at the same time, because then this may not be an issue.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1648,6 +1648,708 @@ function vim._assert_integer(x, base)
   return vim._tointeger(x, base) or error(('Cannot convert %s to integer'):format(x))
 end
 
+--- Returns whether or not the given integer is even.
+---
+--- @param n integer The integer to check for parity.
+--- @return boolean parity The parity.
+function vim.isEven(n)
+  n = vim._assert_integer(n)
+
+  -- Convert negative integer to positive in O(2) time.
+  if vim.startswith(tostring(n), '-') then
+    n = tonumber(tostring(n):sub(2)) --[[@as integer]]
+  end
+
+  -- TODO(ribru17): Eventually we will need vim.isOdd(), but for now a viable workaround is to
+  -- use `vim.isEven(n - 1)`.

comment_data.body: Just add `if gonna_be_wrong_due_to_floating_point_error() then dont() end` to the code

@@ -1648,6 +1648,708 @@ function vim._assert_integer(x, base)

@@ -1752,6 +1752,15 @@ vim.isarray({t})                                               *vim.isarray()*
     See also: ~
       • https://github.com/openresty/luajit2#tableisarray
 
+vim.isEven({n})                                                 *vim.isEven()*
+    Returns whether or not the given integer is even.
+
+    Parameters: ~
+      • {n}  (`integer`) The integer to check for parity.
+
+    Return: ~
+        (`boolean`) parity The parity.
+

comment_data.body: Tbh this looks more like an odd number. Might even be the answer to a much broader question... Oh well

comment_regular_data.body: having `vim.isEven()` but not `vim.isOdd()` feels a little odd.
Should be in C for better performance.

> Should be in C for better performance.

C isn't good enough. We need to write it in Rust so it's blazingly fast.
now this is an odd pull request
lgtm, i say it should be merged
why reinvent the wheel instead of using the npm package directly?

> why reinvent the wheel instead of using the npm package directly?

in this case we should download it from npm at request time. If the spec evolves, it will keep us up to date (and prevent all security issues...)
Closing until we find a solution using the npm package

> now this is an odd pull request

no, its an even pr

title: feet(lua): vim.isEven()
